result:
All 10 call sites accounted for. Summary:

## (a) Locally freed via `decrRefCount` in the same function — 6 sites

| Line | Function | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| 197 | `setGenericCommand` | `milliseconds_obj` used in `rewriteClientCommandArgument`/`rewriteClientCommandVector`, freed at line 207 |
| 541 | `getexCommand` | `milliseconds_obj` used in `rewriteClientCommandVector`, freed at line 543 |
| 891 | `msetGenericCommand`/`msetCommand`-family | `milliseconds_obj` used in `rewriteClientCommandArgument` (x2), freed at line 894 |
| 1345 | `incrExGeneric`/`increxCommand` | `milliseconds_obj` used in `rewriteClientCommandVector`, freed at line 1347 |
| 1439 | `lcsCommand` | `createStringObject("",0)` for missing key `a`; freed via `goto cleanup` → `decrRefCount(obja)` at line 1629 |
| 1440 | `lcsCommand` | `createStringObject("",0)` for missing key `b`; freed via `goto cleanup` → `decrRefCount(objb)` at line 1630 |

All six are transient "millisecond timestamp" or "empty-string" scratch objects used only to build a rewritten command / for read-only string comparison, then explicitly released before the function returns.

## (b) Ownership transferred elsewhere, no local `decrRefCount` — 4 sites

| Line | Function | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| 926 | `incrDecrCommand` | `new = createStringObjectFromLongLongForValue(value)` handed to `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink`, which install it as the new DB value (via the `&new` out-param link); no local free |
| 983 | `incrbyfloatCommand` | `new = createStringObjectFromLongDouble(...)` handed to `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink`; later only read (`addReplyBulk`, `rewriteClientCommandArgument`), never decremented |
| 1323 | `incrExGeneric` | `new = createStringObjectFromLongDouble(value_ld,1)` (float branch) handed to `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink` |
| 1325 | `incrExGeneric` | `new = createStringObjectFromLongLongForValue(value_ll)` (int branch) handed to `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink` |

All four are the "new value" objects for INCR/INCRBY/INCRBYFLOAT/INCREX-family commands: the pointer is stored into `&new`/db via `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink`, and the DB now owns the single reference — the function only reads it afterward (`addReplyLongLongFromStr`, `addReplyBulk`, `rewriteClientCommandVector`, `keyModified`) and never calls `decrRefCount` on it.

Method: `grep -n` for all four function names in `src/t_string.c` (10 hits, matches manual read-through count), then `Read` each surrounding function body to trace the local variable to either a `decrRefCount` call or a `dbReplaceValueWithLink`/`dbAddByLink` ownership-transfer call. No LSP navigation was strictly needed here since all call sites and their fates are within the single file and traceable by direct reading; grep coverage and manual reading were used as the primary method, cross-checked against the file's own `cleanup:` label for the LCS case.
